title: IMGUI Debugging
use_for: diagnosing IMGUI event-flow, focus, and layout problems
adding instrumentation for repaint, control, and routing issues
making UI framework behavior reviewable during development
rules: instrument event flow before rewriting architecture
trace focus and control ownership at framework boundaries
prefer compact diagnostics that are cheap to disable
make debugging tools align with host, panel, and widget boundaries
procedure: Identify the failing event, focus, or layout behavior.
Add tracing at the host and boundary levels before leaf widgets.
Record layout or repaint counts and event consumption decisions.
Use the trace to isolate ownership rather than guessing.

primary_when: the request is specifically about debugging focus, event consumption, repaint behavior, or layout mismatches in IMGUI
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about the initial architecture of the framework

solves: how to debug invisible IMGUI ownership and event-flow failures
how to add useful traces without overwhelming the UI framework

core_principles: Prefer traces that reveal ownership transitions over noisy per-widget logs.
Keep diagnostics cheap enough to leave available during active development.
constraints: Instrumentation must not change the control structure it is observing.
Tracing should be correlated to concrete windows, panels, and event phases.
Diagnostic overlays must not become new hidden owners of focus.
decision_rules: Instrument host-level event routing before inspecting leaf widgets.
Add layout mismatch diagnostics when structure differs between layout and repaint.
Trace focus changes whenever keyboard handling appears nondeterministic.

preferred_patterns: compact counters for repaint and layout frequency
opt-in overlays for state and focus debugging
avoid_patterns: diagnostics coupled to feature business logic
debug helpers that consume the events they inspect

recipes.name: Investigate a focus bug in a multi-panel tool window
recipes.when: Use when keyboard input seems to jump between panels unpredictably.
steps: Trace focus ownership at the host boundary.
Record the active panel and consumed events.
Compare transitions across consecutive IMGUI events until the owner mismatch is visible.

pitfalls: Adding logging so deep that the actual owner boundary stays invisible.
Using diagnostics that alter timing or event consumption.
Capturing too much data to see the routing mistake clearly.
review_checklist: Do traces start at host boundaries?
Are focus and consumed events visible?
Can repaint or layout anomalies be counted?
Are debug tools passive relative to the event flow?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Debugging
references.interpretation: Use this first for tracing ownership, focus bugs, and layout anomalies from framework boundaries inward.
references.why: Use for tracing event ownership, repaint/layout anomalies, and focus bugs at framework boundaries.

references.title: Unity Scripting API: Event
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm event inspection details after the domain's debugging workflow is established.
references.why: Use for event inspection details when designing event and focus tracing.
